topic: pivot table question — summarizing transaction data

turns.content: okay so I have a quick Excel question if you dont mind. I need to build a pivot table but I keep getting it wrong and its doing my head in
Of course, happy to help. What data are you working with, and what are you trying to summarize?

so I have a big spreadsheet of transaction data. columns are like date, client name, transaction type (acquisition, disposal, restructuring etc), deal value, and sector. theres about 400 rows. I need to summarise total deal value by sector and transaction type

That's a pretty standard pivot table setup. Here's what you want to do:

1. Select your entire data range (all 400 rows including headers)
2. Go to Insert > PivotTable
3. Put it on a new worksheet
4. Drag **Sector** to the Rows area
5. Drag **Transaction Type** to the Columns area
6. Drag **Deal Value** to the Values area — it should automatically sum it

That'll give you a grid with sectors down the side, transaction types across the top, and summed deal values in the cells.
okay wait I did that but the deal value column is showing as Count instead of Sum?? like it says 'Count of Deal Value' and its giving me numbers like 12, 34 instead of the actual monetary amounts

That usually happens when some of your Deal Value cells contain text or blanks instead of numbers. Excel defaults to Count when it detects non-numeric data. Two things to try:

1. Click on "Count of Deal Value" in the Values area of the PivotTable Fields pane, then click "Value Field Settings" and change it from Count to Sum.
2. Go back to your raw data and check the Deal Value column — look for any cells that have dashes, "N/A", text, or are blank. Those are probably causing it.
omg wait. there are some rows where deal value says 'TBC' because the value hasnt been confirmed yet. thats probably it init

That's exactly it. Excel sees "TBC" and treats the whole column as mixed data, so it defaults to Count. You've got a few options:

- Replace "TBC" with blank cells (just delete the text) — the pivot table will then ignore those rows in the sum
- Replace "TBC" with 0 if you want them included as zero-value transactions
- Or filter them out of the pivot table entirely using a filter on Deal Value

I'd probably go with blanks if you don't want them skewing your totals, since putting 0 might make it look like those deals are worthless rather than just unconfirmed.
yeah blanks make more sense. let me do a find and replace for TBC. okay done — and now its showing Sum of Deal Value. you legend

Nice one! Now you should have a clean summary. A couple of extra tips that might be useful:

- Right-click any value in the pivot table and go to Number Format to add currency formatting (£) if it's not already showing
- You can add a filter at the top if you want to look at specific date ranges — just drag the Date field to the Filters area
ooh the date filter thing is useful actually. can I group the dates by quarter? like I want to see Q1 vs Q2 vs Q3 vs Q4 instead of every individual date

Yes! Move the Date field from Filters into the Rows area (you can put it above or below Sector). Then right-click on any date in the pivot table, select "Group," and you'll get options to group by Months, Quarters, Years, etc. Tick Quarters and Years, untick everything else. That'll collapse all your dates into Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4 broken out by year.

that is so much cleaner than what I was doing before. I was literally using SUMIFS formulas to manually calculate each quarter and it was taking forever loool. pivot tables are actually mad when you know how to use them

They really are — once you get comfortable with them, you'll use them for everything. They're so much faster than SUMIFS for this kind of summary work. And if someone asks you to slice the data a different way, you just drag fields around instead of rewriting formulas. One more thing: if you want it to look presentable, go to the Design tab while the pivot table is selected and pick a style. Makes it look polished with minimal effort.
perfect. gonna make this look all professional before I send it round. thanks so much honestly that saved me like an hour of messing about
Glad I could help! Once you send it, if anyone asks for a different view of the same data, just duplicate the sheet and rearrange the pivot table instead of starting from scratch. Good luck with it.
